feed.title: SAP Community - PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance
feed.subtitle: PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Proactive Cross Plant Planning in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi PM Community,

We are in a situation in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition;

Master Data maintained in Technical Object(Equipment)

Planning Plant 1001

Maintenance Work Center is 1003PM

Work Center Plant is 1003

When the Maintenance Item is being created we get this error;


If we change manually the Main Work Center Plant into 1003, then the error message get's fixed and item can be saved.

If we change the Planning Plant for technical object into 1003, then the Main Work Center Plant is 1003 in the Maintenance Item, so it fetches the Main Work Center Plant behind the Planning Plant.

In the configuration Planning Plant for Plant 1003, is 1003.

Is this a bug in system, is there something we should not maintain in the technical object master data or configuration issue?

What would be the solution that the data inside the maintenance item doesn't need to be performed manually?

entry.title: Scope of SAP pm module
entry.content: Hi sap community, i am new to SAP Plant maintenance module. I have made the following document with contents of SAP Plant maintenance module, can you please review this document and let me know if there is any room for improvementhttps://hclo365-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/personal/sukhman-singh_hcltech_com/Documents/sap%20pm.docx?d=w877a263e7bb44f759c0589dd326aee86&csf=1&web=1&e=mJMj53 .

entry.title: How to add a check for requisitioner for purchase requisition created from plant maintenance orders?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We have a requirement to add a validation for the requisitioner field before creating Purchase requisition(s) from a Plant maintenance order. The Purchase requisition(s) could be created immediately from operation lines or external component lines while creating and saving the Plant maintenance order.

Requirement:

  • Restrict the saving of the PM order and the creation of the Purchase requisition if the validation fails with an error.
  • Return to the order creation/ change screen (IW31/ IW32) in case of error (error shouldn't terminate current session)
  • Allow the data in the PM order to be edited (error shouldn't block editing)
  • We need an enhancement that has all the necessary inputs for operation lines and components and allow to restrict the commit for saving the PM order and creation of the purchase requisition

What we have checked so far:

  • I tried to check the WORKORDER_UPDATE BAdI using the method AT_SAVE, but this method lacks the information on the current operation lines and the components as inputs.
  • Then we tried adding the validating in BEFORE_UPDATE method of WORKORDER_UPDATE BAdI, but this method gets called after the AT_SAVE method, so it doesn't serve the purpose.

If anyone has any experience with handling such validation, please share your inputs. Thanks in advance!

Regards,
Aavil

entry.title: SAP EAM - multiple checklists per maintenance order operation
entry.content:
Greetings,

we are wondering if it is possible to implement multiple checklist per maintenance order operation. We are aware of the possibility of having multiple checklists per maintenance order - one per operation.

We have tried the following:

  • adding multiple operating in a single inspection plan - in this example characteristics from all operations get joined in one checklist
  • classifying multiple inspection plans with the same checklist type value - the system created a checklists for the last valid inspection plan and ignores the others

We have a business scenario where we have a long checklist and we would like to structure it as best as possible to reduce administrative overhead. If we use "Perform maintenance jobs" to record results we would like to enter into all checklists from as few operations as possible - preferably 1.

Unfortunately due to the long list of characteristics we cannot group them all in one checklist because the list is just too long and not user friendly with a big probability for errors.

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Script history
entry.content:
Good morning!

I have a question about SAP PM:

I need to create a feature to view the change history of Routes created in CA21, modified in CA22, and viewed in CA23.

This was the status before my attempt.

I created a new status with the new configuration.

I created a new usage and a new status, but I couldn't generate a change history in the script.

Can someone help me?

entry.title: Reference Equipment or Copy Option Not Visible While Creating Equipment
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We are trying to create equipment using the Reference Equipment option. However, in our system the Reference Equipment or Copy option is not visible while creating the equipment.

Because of this, we are unable to create new equipment using an existing equipment as a reference.

Advance Thanks

entry.title: Mass update Moving Average Price in Material
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Can you please let me know if there is a way to mass update moving average price for multiple materials.

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Create multiple task lists with service packages, with CALL FUNCTION 'EAM_TASKLIST_CREATE'
entry.content:
Dear all,

I need a simple example how to create 2 task lists with service packages via fm  'EAM_TASKLIST_CREATE' inside a loop.

entry.title: Rescheduling of maintenance orders after breakdown
entry.content:
Hi!

I've got a question about rescheduling orders after a breakdown.

Let's say we have the following scheduling, calendar day based, cycle is 3 months.

As we can see, the called order is scheduled for 03.19, and this is the same day the next order gets called as the call horizon is 0%.

Let's say we have a breakdown of this part on 02.10, so maintenance gets carried out. Now, we obviously dont want to perform the task on 03.19., so we change the basic start-finish dates of the maintenance order in transaction IW38 to approx. 05.10.

Is this the correct procedure, and will this change the call dates of the following orders aswell? Also, is this procedure correct for counter based maintenance aswell?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: sap business one b1 plant maintenance export
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to export the data of Work Orders and Diagnostic Sheets from the Plant Maintenance module in SAP Business One (B1) to an Excel file.
Please send me the procedure to do this operation.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Maintenance Order Object list change documents log
entry.content:
Hello Team, 

Looking forward for support to fetch the information from SAP Tables or FM details for the below issue: 

1. Maintenance Order is created through PM Plan with standard Object list information fetch from maintenance item (4 - Equipment's object list is maintained in Maint. Item - MPOS).

2. Maintenance order - Object list is observed with 127 Equipment's list derived automatically without any change log or document history in maintenance tables. 

3. I have tried mutliple tables and FM, Couldn't exactly trace out the issue with from where additional object list equipments are appeared in maintenance order. 

Appreciate your support with help of any exact FM or table details. 

PLM Enterprise Asset Management (EAM)/Plant Maintenance (PM) 

Regards, 

Vishal R

SAP EAM Consultant

entry.title: Failed work orders / Why have maintenance plans stopped generating work orders?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Masters,
I recently noticed that some maintenance plans had stopped generating work orders, even though they were being generated correctly until last year: why is this happening?

When I analysed this in detail, I even noticed that two maintenance plans
> created in exactly the same way, on the same day, with only a 30-day difference in the issuance of work orders.
... only maintenance plan ‘A’ was still generating its work orders, whilst ‘B’ had stopped

How is this possible??

Or, what should I check in the completed work orders?

Or in the maintenance plans?? Although, as I said: they are identical!

Thank you for your help in resolving this issue 🙂

entry.title: Serial Number Profile Change
entry.content:
Hi Team,

What will happen if we add serialization procedure POSL to serial number profile for existing materials? What is the best approach to do this?

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: SAP PS Project system - display costs under maintenance order level
entry.content:
Greetings,

we are wondering if it possible to display costs in project under maintenance order level using transaction CN41N. We have several maintenance orders linked to WBS elements. These orders have different costs planned:

  • internal labour
  • external service
  • stock material
  • non stock material

We are able to see costs in transaction CN41N but only until maintenance order level. Below all values are 0.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Serial Number is locked by User error in MIGO
entry.content:
Hi Team,

For Refurbishment work order process when we post Transfer Posting  and immediately post Goods Receipt in MIGO for same purchase order then we get an error as "Serial Number is already locked by user". What is the Cause and how to fix it?

Note: If we do Transfer Posting and do Goods Receipt after 5 mins then there is no issue.

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Technical Object Breakdowns - relationship Comp code & Maintenance Plants missing per design or gap?
entry.content:
Noticed no data shown when opening Technical Object Breakdowns - S/4HANA Cloud. Even when selecting all maint plants. Already found the rootcause and the workaround (see below) but do not like it from a user experience.

Apparently, there’s no association with Technical object and Company Code and that’s causing below. Is that as per design and something we need to live with or something we’re missing in the dataloads?

We advise users to set default setting so they do not need to fill in e.g. company code each and every time in each app (unfortunate btw this can not be defaulted centrally for all endusers)

Clearing comp code

entry.title: How to force measurement point entry in PM order confirmation?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

we assigned measuerment points to some of our functional locations to track run time for liquids ran through a pipe for example.

Is there a possibility to force the user to enter actual status of the measurement point (actual counter) during PM order confirmation or at least during completion of the PM order?

We have S4CORE (SAPK-10701INS4CORE).

As of now I did not found anything excepts development what I cannot believe that this is the only way.

thanks in advance.

Best regards

Patrick

entry.title: Asset Viewer and Asset Overview app gaps
entry.content:
2 questions related to 2 critical apps for EAM

Asset Viewer app

why is navigation window so super small at the left and is so much space blank at the right. from a UX perspective this is far from useless for endusers. hope its a quick win to improve by expanding the window at the left at least 

Asset Overview app

this new 2602 was highly anticipated within our organisation to remove some of the gaps so far between Public Cloud and our original ECC setup. several asks 

  • Why not possible to expand to full view technical object, equipment, BOM, material. See Asset viewer technical object as reference.
  • why so limited amount of columns/slicers available 
  • link to measurement points history across the technical object or at individual equipment levels in the roadmap? 
  • Cumulative risk visual in the roadmap? 

keen to hear how other companies cope with this and to hear from SAP when/what will be changed in these 2 apps. Are last 2 bullets covered by different SAP products like APM or anticipated to have this also within Public Cloud EAM as basic functionality?

entry.title: Change workorder access removed since mid march
entry.content:
Since mid march we have more and more of our front line users complaining they can not all of a sudden edit workorders anymore. We have thousands of workorders each year and 90% of our users are EAM users working with workorders day in day out. Did notice on the 15th SAP ALM changes. Did SAP incorrectly change authorizations? More clients observing the same and more importantly- found a solution?

Heard someone mentioning it can be caused by fields added with Extensibility but that would suprise me a thats a clean core pattern. New /updated apps a cause???

entry.title: SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant Asset Management (EAM)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I recently tried 1 attempt to my Exame to become a SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Asset Management, my score was 50% and the cut score is 70%. It was my first time trying this kind of exam and the system does not give you a feedback where you should improve or what part of the exam you was worse. I'd like to know if some of you have tried the new format exam and how was your experience? If I perform more things that the exame ask me to do, Will I be penalized?
Best regards.
